acceptedConstraints.summary: The current derivation still requires admin-shell, user-management, and role-permission-management as the stable core.
acceptedConstraints.effect: The standalone app-manifest must keep the core-admin module set intact.

acceptedConstraints.summary: The planning output already excludes project-management, project-repository-management, kanban-management, and ticket-management for the initial derivation.
acceptedConstraints.effect: Those delivery-management modules must remain out of the manifest unless planning is regenerated.

basis.summary: The core-admin profile exactly matches the deterministic planning output.
The planning output resolves every required manifest field and leaves no unresolved conflicts.

prerequisites: Keep admin-shell, user-management, and role-permission-management in the manifest
Leave the excluded delivery-management modules deferred for this initial derivation
boundaryNotes: This is optional guidance. Contributors may choose an equivalent standalone manifest as long as accepted constraints stay intact.

basis.summary: The current business scope explicitly excludes the delivery-management module family.
Project-management is the dependency root for repository, kanban, and ticket workflows.

prerequisites: Regenerate the planning artifact before reintroducing project-management or any downstream delivery module
boundaryNotes: Because this recommendation is constraint-derived, contributors should not override it by editing the recommendation artifact alone.

notes: Produce a standalone app-manifest instead of passing planning or recommendation artifacts into assembly tooling.
If a contributor deviates from heuristic guidance, they should record the reason next to the manifest source.

followUpGuidance.guidance: If a contributor accepts different optional guidance than the repository recommendation, they SHOULD document which planning facts justify that manifest change before assembly.
If a contributor wants to violate an accepted constraint, they MUST regenerate the planning artifact instead of editing the recommendation only.

manifestPreparationBoundary.nextStep: Review the recommendation artifact, keep accepted constraints intact, decide whether to follow the suggested core-admin profile, and then create a standalone app-manifest before invoking ./scripts/platform-tool.sh assembly scaffold.
